description: Single source of truth for the voice tell-set. Surface + rhythm + King craft + Russian + Hebrew tells. Profiles reference tells by `id`; the linter resolves `detector` + `method` from here. See SCHEMA.md for the contract. Constitution: the linter core is regex+stat only:`method: "llm-only"` tells are NOT run by `tacheles check`; they belong to the rewrite/King procedure layer (see ../anchor/voice-anchor.md).

methods.regex: deterministic pattern match, runs in the linter core
methods.stat: deterministic statistic over sentence/paragraph signal, runs in the linter core
methods.llm-only: irreducibly semantic, NOT in the linter core; handled by the rewrite/King procedure layer

status.stub: detector exists but returns [], needs revival (src/detectors/disabled.ts)
status.planned: no detector yet, to be built in step 3

tells.title: Inline colon-header
tells.summary: `word:` or `**word:**` + description used as an inline label. Strongest Pangram signal.
tells.bad: Context: the team had shipped on Friday. Fix: we rolled back.
tells.fix: Fold the label into running prose.

tells.title: Rule-of-three list
tells.summary: Three parallel items in a comma list or three matched bullets/adjectives.
tells.bad: efficient, reliable, and effective
tells.fix: Use two or four items. If a triplet fits naturally, drop one.

tells.title: Balanced both-sides hedge
tells.summary: Refuses to commit: 'both can be true depending on', 'there's truth on both sides', 'it depends'.
tells.bad: Both can be true depending on your threat model.
tells.fix: Pick one side or state the disagreement.

tells.title: Bolded inline list header
tells.summary: List items that open with a bolded label then a description.
tells.bad: - **Speed:** it ran in 2s.
tells.fix: Plain bullets with running prose.

tells.title: Boldface mid-prose
tells.summary: Bold used to emphasize a phrase inside a paragraph.
tells.bad: This is the **single most important** thing.
tells.fix: Let the sentence carry the emphasis; drop the bold.

tells.title: Transition-word stack
tells.summary: More than one of {furthermore, moreover, additionally, however, nevertheless} per 500 words.
tells.bad: Furthermore... Moreover... Additionally...
tells.fix: Max one per 500 words; usually cut entirely.

tells.title: Meta-scaffolding
tells.summary: Announcing the structure: 'In this piece I will', 'Let's dive in', 'First, let's explore'.
tells.bad: In this post, we'll explore three ways to...
tells.fix: Structure is felt, not signposted (McPhee). Just start.

tells.title: Non-ASCII punctuation
tells.summary: Curly quotes, arrows, em-dashes, smart apostrophes, ASCII/Cyrillic hyphen confusables. Copy-paste-from-elsewhere signal.
tells.bad: “the fix” — it worked → done
tells.fix: ASCII only in plaintext channels (forums, chat). NOT applicable to DOCX/PDF deliverables.

tells.title: AI vocabulary
tells.summary: Kill-list: leverage, utilize, robust, comprehensive, seamless, delve, realm, tapestry, landscape, journey, cornerstone, pivotal, meticulous, intricate, underscores, multifaceted, transformative, innovative, foster, harness, streamline, facilitate, commence, elevate.
tells.bad: leverage robust frameworks to foster a seamless journey
tells.fix: Anglo-Saxon over Latinate: use/start/help/try. Name the specific thing, not the category.

tells.title: Inflated copula
tells.summary: 'serves as', 'represents', 'stands as', 'marks', 'is a testament to' instead of plain 'is'.
tells.bad: The framework serves as a cornerstone.
tells.fix: Use 'is', or name what it does.

tells.title: Em-dash overuse
tells.summary: Em-dashes above human baseline (~3.23/1000 words; Claude runs 10+).
tells.bad: It worked — mostly — and then — well — it didn't.
tells.fix: Reserve for genuine interruption; ~1 per 400 words. Use a comma or period.

tells.title: Rhetorical question + one-word answer
tells.summary: 'The result? Resilience.' Performs drama without delivering it.
tells.bad: The catch? Cost. The kicker? It scales.
tells.fix: Answer in prose, or don't frame it as a question.

tells.title: Hedge / filler opener
tells.summary: 'It's worth noting', "I'd be happy to", 'Great question', 'Certainly', 'That being said'.
tells.bad: It's worth noting that the vendor shipped defaults.
tells.fix: Delete the windup; lead with the claim.

tells.title: Significance wrap (closer)
tells.summary: Section-closing significance assertion: 'This underscores the importance of', 'is a testament to', 'sets the stage for'. Zero new information.
tells.bad: This underscores the critical importance of proactive resilience.
tells.fix: Cut it. Let the content carry its weight; end on a fact.

tells.title: Reframe opener / antithesis
tells.summary: Define then negate the opposite: 'not X but Y', 'It's not X, it's Y', 'not out of malice, out of token pressure'.
tells.bad: Cost is a measured number here, not a guess.
tells.fix: Commit to Y directly. Allowed once per piece at the moment that earns it.
tells.source: rhythm tell #1; Gorrie; revive disabled.ts stub

params.anchorRe: ^(Not|It's not|This isn't|The right question is not)

tells.title: Significance announcement (opener)
tells.summary: Telling the reader it's interesting before saying it: 'here's the thing', 'the interesting part is', 'the honest picture is', 'clear and a little surprising'.
tells.bad: The shape is clear and a little surprising:
tells.fix: Just state the thing. Let the reader decide it's surprising.
tells.source: rhythm tell #2; Hyland metadiscourse + P41 wordlist

tells.title: Bold aphorism
tells.summary: A standalone bolded crafted claim presented as a revelation. Markdown-tractable: a `**...**` span that is its own line/sentence.
tells.bad: **wall clock ≈ tokens ≈ round trips.**
tells.fix: Make the claim in plain prose inside a paragraph, or cut it.

tells.title: Italic for drama
tells.summary: Single-word italic stress mid-sentence: `*is*`, `_the_`.
tells.bad: The subprocess time *is* the wall clock.
tells.fix: Let word order carry the stress; drop the italic.

tells.title: Sentence-parallel triad
tells.summary: Three consecutive sentences/clauses with shared opening (anaphora) or matched structure. 'It reports... It marks... It writes...'
tells.bad: It reports the result. It marks the failure. It writes the log.
tells.fix: Break the parallel; vary two of the three.
tells.source: rhythm tell #5; anaphora token-compare across 3 sentences

tells.title: Per-paragraph closing nugget
tells.summary: Every paragraph lands a wisdom mic-drop (King's darling): short, declarative, no connective, pattern repeats across paragraphs. Linter detects the DISTRIBUTIONAL pattern; deciding a given line is a crafted darling vs an earned conclusion is semantic (see llm-only note).
tells.bad: They are the same lever.
tells.fix: Kill the darling. End the paragraph on a concrete detail or a flat statement.
tells.source: rhythm tell #6; revive disabled.ts stub; semantic call -> r-closing-nugget-semantic

tells.title: Uniform sentence/paragraph polish
tells.summary: Low variance in sentence/paragraph length and complexity. Real humans drop clunky/short/throwaway lines; the uniformity itself is the tell. Extend the existing paragraph-length detector with finite-size-corrected burstiness A_n + 2nd-order-diff over sentence-length signal.
tells.bad: Three consecutive 18-word, equally-polished sentences.
tells.fix: Deliberately vary length; leave at least one short/flat line per few paragraphs.
tells.source: rhythm tell #7; Kim-Jo A_n (arXiv:1604.01125); DivEye 2nd-order-diff idea

tells.title: King: -ly adverbs
tells.summary: 'The road to hell is paved with adverbs.' -ly adverbs, especially modifying verbs.
tells.bad: significantly improved, rapidly evolving, fundamentally misunderstood
tells.fix: Delete it. If the sentence needs it, the verb is wrong or you need a concrete detail. 'significantly improved' -> name the number.

tells.title: King: passive voice
tells.summary: was/were/been + past participle (esp. 'be Xed by') that hides the actor. 'Passive voice seems to have been created with the timid writer in mind.'
tells.bad: 40GB was exfiltrated. Mistakes were made.
tells.fix: Name the actor. 'Attackers exfiltrated 40GB.'

tells.title: King: fancy long word
tells.summary: A long/Latinate word where a common short one exists (emolument->tip). 'Use the first word that comes to mind if it is appropriate.' Overlaps s-banned-vocab; this is the general short-word rule.
tells.bad: utilize, commence, facilitate, endeavor, ascertain
tells.fix: use, start, help, try, find out

tells.title: King: clichéd simile
tells.summary: Dead figurative language from a blocklist.
tells.bad: like a madman, pretty as a summer day, fought like a tiger, at the speed of light
tells.fix: Fresh, simple, concrete image, or none.

list: like a kid in a candy store
light at the end of the tunnel

tells.title: King: pet-peeve phrases
tells.summary: Empty bureaucratic filler.
tells.bad: at this point in time, at the end of the day, the fact that, along these lines
tells.fix: Cut, or replace with the plain word (now / because).

list: at the end of the day
when all is said and done

tells.title: King: 2nd-draft 10% cut
tells.summary: 2nd draft should be <= 90% of 1st-draft word count. A draft-delta metric, not a single-file lint; off by default in `tacheles check` (stays out of the single-text DETECTORS dispatch), surfaced via the `tacheles compare-drafts <orig> <rewrite>` subcommand and the rewrite procedure.
tells.bad: 2nd draft same length as 1st.
tells.fix: Find the restatement sentence in each paragraph; cut it.
tells.source: King On Writing D12 / R2; maps to r-closing-nugget

tells.title: Hollow filler phrase
tells.summary: LLM padding phrases that carry no meaning ('вода' / тахлес): it's important to note, when it comes to, in order to, the fact that, plays a crucial role, a wide range of, first and foremost. Delete or replace with the specific point.
tells.bad: It's important to note that, when it comes to security, a number of controls play a crucial role.
tells.fix: Cut the phrase; state the point directly. 'in order to' -> 'to'; 'the fact that' -> 'that'.
tells.source: customization Pack 1 (тахлес); Ilyahov вода / Nora Gal канцелярит

params.label: hollow filler
list: at the end of the day

tells.title: Hollow intensifier
tells.summary: Non-load-bearing intensifiers (very, quite, rather, somewhat). Common in normal prose, so off by default in profiles, opt-in. The -ly intensifiers (basically, essentially, significantly) are already caught by k-adverbs.
tells.bad: This is very important and quite robust.
tells.fix: Delete, or use a stronger specific word.

tells.title: Expletive / ballast opener
tells.summary: Padding constructions 'there is/are X that ...' and 'it is X that ...' that defer the real subject behind ballast. Front the actual subject instead.
tells.bad: There are many teams that struggle. It is the judgment that matters.
tells.fix: 'There are teams that struggle' -> 'Teams struggle'. Front the subject.

tells.title: GPT-leaning vocabulary
tells.summary: GPT-distinctive buzz-vocab/power-verbs not in s-banned-vocab (model-leaning, not exclusive).
tells.bad: a cutting-edge, game-changing platform to supercharge and unlock value
tells.fix: name the concrete thing it does.

tells.title: GPT filler phrases
tells.summary: Padding phrases distinct from s-hollow-filler's set.
tells.bad: Moving forward, the bottom line is, in terms of value...
tells.fix: cut; state the point.

tells.title: Whether-you're pseudo-inclusivity
tells.summary: 'Whether you're X or Y' fake-audience-inclusivity opener, near-exclusively LLM.
tells.bad: Whether you're a startup or an enterprise, ...
tells.fix: address one reader directly.

patterns.message: 'Whether you're X or Y' pseudo-inclusivity opener

tells.title: GPT scaffolding constructions
tells.summary: Stock GPT openers/pivots: in-today's-landscape, imagine/picture-this, let's-dive, by-VERBing, at-its-core.
tells.bad: In today's digital landscape... Let's dive in. At its core...
tells.fix: open on a concrete fact.

patterns.message: 'At its core / when you strip it' pseudo-depth

tells.title: Vague future-facing close
tells.summary: Empty forward-looking sign-off.
tells.bad: As we look ahead, the future of work is bright. Only time will tell.
tells.fix: end on a concrete claim or cut.

tells.title: Канцелярит (отымённые предлоги, расщеплённое сказуемое)
tells.summary: Нора Галь / Главред: канцелярские отымённые предлоги ('в целях', 'в части', 'в рамках') и связка 'является' / расщеплённое сказуемое: мёртвый официально-деловой слой. Cyrillic-bounded so it does not match inside larger words.
tells.bad: В целях обеспечения безопасности данный продукт является решением.
tells.fix: Замени на живой глагол: 'чтобы защитить … продукт защищает'. Убери 'является' — поставь тире или прямое сказуемое.
tells.source: Нора Галь «Слово живое и мёртвое»; Ильяхов/Сарычева «Пиши, сокращай»; glvrd.ru

patterns.re: (?<![А-Яа-яЁёA-Za-z])(?:в целях|в части|в рамках|в связи с|в случае|при наличии|при условии|в отношении|в соответствии с|на основании|в течение|в ходе|посредством|в плане|по вопросу)(?![А-Яа-яЁёA-Za-z])

tells.title: Оценки и усилители без фактов
tells.summary: Ильяхов: оценочные прилагательные и усилители, которыми ИИ-текст подменяет факты ('уникальный', 'инновационный', 'потрясающий', 'крайне'). 'очень' намеренно НЕ включено: это нормальная разговорная частотность живой речи.
tells.bad: Это уникальное, инновационное и крайне потрясающее решение.
tells.fix: Убери оценку, покажи факт: что именно делает и насколько (число, пример).

tells.title: Штампы и клише
tells.summary: Газетно-сетевые штампы, которыми ИИ заполняет пустоту ('в современном мире', 'на сегодняшний день', 'играет важную роль', 'цифровая трансформация').
tells.bad: В современном мире цифровая трансформация играет важную роль.
tells.fix: Вырежи штамп и скажи конкретно: кто, что и зачем делает.

patterns.re: (?<![А-Яа-яЁёA-Za-z])(?:в современном мире|в наше время|на сегодняшний день|ни для кого не секрет|не секрет,? что|трудно переоценить|играет (?:важн|ключев|значим)[а-яё]+ роль|имеет (?:большое|важное|огромное) значение|в эпоху цифров[а-яё]+|цифров(?:ая|ой) трансформаци[а-яё]*|в условиях современн[а-яё]+|идти в ногу со временем|выйти на новый уровень|на острие)(?![А-Яа-яЁёA-Za-z])

tells.title: Вводная вода
tells.summary: Вводные конструкции-вода, которые ничего не добавляют ('стоит отметить', 'важно понимать', 'как известно'), типичный наполнитель ИИ-абзацев.
tells.bad: Стоит отметить, что, как известно, важно понимать суть.
tells.fix: Убери зачин — сразу скажи суть.

patterns.re: (?<![А-Яа-яЁёA-Za-z])(?:стоит отметить|следует (?:отметить|подчеркнуть|сказать)|важно (?:понимать|отметить|помнить)|как известно|необходимо отметить|хотелось бы отметить|нельзя не (?:отметить|сказать)|само собой разумеется|не лишним будет|заметим,? что)(?![А-Яа-яЁёA-Za-z])

tells.title: ИИ-зачины и концовки (RU)
tells.summary: Шаблонные конструкции ChatGPT/LLM на русском: зачины 'давайте разберёмся' / 'в этой статье мы рассмотрим' и концовки 'в заключение' / 'подведём итог'. Привязаны к границе предложения.
tells.bad: Давайте разберёмся. ... В заключение стоит отметить. Подведём итог.
tells.fix: Начни с конкретного факта; закончи выводом, а не объявлением вывода.

tells.title: הגזמות ומילות הערכה ללא עובדות
tells.summary: Hebrew hype adjectives that AI text uses in place of facts (מהפכני 'revolutionary', פורץ דרך 'groundbreaking', עוצמתי 'powerful'). Hebrew-letter boundaries allow attached prefixes (ו/ה/ב/ל/מ/ש). Deliberately EXCLUDES ייחודי/חדשני/מתקדם, they occur in clean human-authored corpora (natural usage). SEED list: a native Hebrew speaker should review/expand.
tells.bad: פתרון מהפכני, עוצמתי ופורץ דרך.
tells.fix: הורד את ההערכה, הצג עובדה: מה בדיוק עושה ובכמה.
tells.source: off-LM hype-lexicon (RU ru-usiliteli twin); clear-writing anti-cliché

patterns.re: (?<![א-תA-Za-z])[ו]?(?:פורץ דרך|פורצת דרך|חסר תקדים|חסרת תקדים|יוצא דופן|יוצאת דופן|שובר שיאים|בעל ערך רב)(?![א-תA-Za-z])

tells.title: קלישאות וביטויי-מדף
tells.summary: Hebrew clichés AI uses as filler (בעידן הדיגיטלי 'in the digital era', ממלא תפקיד מרכזי 'plays a central role', אין ספק ש 'there's no doubt that'). SEED list: review with native ear.
tells.bad: בעידן הדיגיטלי, אבטחת מידע ממלאת תפקיד מרכזי.
tells.fix: מחק את הקלישאה ואמור מי עושה מה וכמה.

patterns.re: (?<![א-תA-Za-z])[ו]?(?:בעידן הדיגיטלי|בעולם של היום|בעידן המודרני|בעידן הנוכחי|בעל חשיבות רבה|חלק בלתי נפרד|אין ספק ש|ממלא תפקיד (?:מרכזי|חשוב|מפתח)|ממלאת תפקיד (?:מרכזי|חשוב|מפתח)|משחק תפקיד מרכזי)(?![א-תA-Za-z])

tells.title: מים ופתיחים ריקים
tells.summary: Hebrew empty lead-ins that add nothing (חשוב לציין 'important to note', ראוי לציין 'worth noting', כידוע 'as is known'), typical AI paragraph filler.
tells.bad: חשוב לציין שכידוע, ראוי לציין את העניין.
tells.fix: מחק את הפתיח — אמור מיד את העיקר.

tells.title: פתיחים וסיומים של AI (עברית)
tells.summary: Templated ChatGPT/LLM Hebrew openers/closers: 'בואו נצלול/נבחן', 'במאמר זה נבחן', 'בשורה התחתונה', 'לסיכום'. Sentence-bounded where possible.
tells.bad: בואו נצלול לעומק. במאמר זה נבחן. בשורה התחתונה, לסיכום.
tells.fix: פתח בעובדה קונקרטית; סיים במסקנה, לא בהכרזה על מסקנה.
tells.source: off-LM scaffolding-lexicon (RU ru-ai-scaffolding twin); modeltell lets_dive

semanticCompanions.r-closing-nugget-semantic: Deciding whether a closing line is a crafted darling vs an earned conclusion is llm-only (rewrite layer).
semanticCompanions.king-10pct-cut-which-sentence: Which sentence is the restatement to cut needs embeddings/LLM, kept OUT of the linter core (rewrite layer).

deferredKingFictionRules.reason: Fiction-specific; not applicable to CISO/consulting non-fiction. Recorded for completeness, not in the active tell-set.

notes.deferred: Russian (ru) and Hebrew (he) tells, and persona/recipient axes, are out of scope for this build (EN core only). RU anchor research captured in personal-brand memory voice-research-prior-art.md (Ilyahov + Nora Gal).
